days.title: Means of transport: getting from A to B

grammar.title: Naming transport with the verb 'to be'
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian has a present-tense verb 'to be' (съм, си, е, сме, сте, са) — unlike Russian. Use it to describe transport: Колата е нова (The car is new), Влаковете са бързи (The trains are fast). There are no noun cases; the noun keeps one form and the definite article is added as a suffix: кола → колата, влак → влакът. Word order is simple subject + е + adjective.

examples.english: This is a bus.
The car is old.
The trains are fast.

reading.title: На спирката
reading.text: — Това влак ли е? — Не, това е автобус. Аз отивам пеша.
reading.translation: — Is this a train? — No, this is a bus. I am going on foot.

days.speakingPrompt: Name three kinds of transport you can see in your city using 'Това е...' and say which one you usually take.

days.title: Prepositions of place: в, на, до

vocab.exampleEn: The ticket is on the table.
The stop is next to the bank.
The ticket is on the table.
The stop is next to the station.

grammar.title: Showing place with prepositions, not cases
grammar.explanation: Because Bulgarian has no noun cases, place is shown only by prepositions before an unchanged noun. в = in (в колата = in the car), на = on/at (на спирката = at the stop), до = next to (до гарата = next to the station). The noun never changes its ending; only the definite suffix may attach: гара → гарата, влак → влакът.

examples.english: I am in the bus.
The ticket is on the table.
The stop is next to the centre.

reading.title: Къде е спирката?
reading.text: — Къде е спирката? — До гарата. — А гарата е в центъра.
reading.translation: — Where is the stop? — Next to the station. — And the station is in the centre.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe where three places are near you using в, на and до (e.g. 'Спирката е до...').

days.title: Movement: from and to (от, за)

vocab.exampleEn: I am going to the station.
I am coming from the centre.

grammar.title: Direction with от (from) and за (to)
grammar.explanation: Movement is also shown by prepositions, never by case endings. от = from a starting point (Идвам от София = I come from Sofia), за = towards a destination (Влакът е за Варна = The train is to Varna). With verbs of motion use the simple present: отивам (I go), идвам (I come). Remember there is no infinitive in Bulgarian — to say 'I want to go' use искам да + present: Искам да отида.

examples.english: I am coming from the centre.
The bus is to Plovdiv.
I want to go to the station.

reading.title: Откъде и закъде
reading.text: — Този влак е от София. — Да, и е за Варна. — Пътят е дълъг.
reading.translation: — This train is from Sofia. — Yes, and it is to Varna. — The trip is long.

days.speakingPrompt: Say where you are coming from and where you are going today using от and за.

days.title: Travelling by: the preposition с

grammar.title: Saying how you travel: с + transport
grammar.explanation: To say by what means you travel, use с (with/by) + the transport noun, with no case change: пътувам с влак (I travel by train), с автобус, с кола, с метро. Compare пеша (on foot), which needs no preposition: отивам пеша. The verbs пътувам (I travel) and карам (I drive) take the present tense directly.

examples.english: I travel by train.
He comes by bus.
We go on foot.

reading.title: С какво пътуваш?
reading.text: — С какво пътуваш? — С влак, бързо е. — А аз отивам пеша.
reading.translation: — What do you travel by? — By train, it's fast. — And I am going on foot.

days.speakingPrompt: Explain how you get to work or school using с + transport, and whether it is бързо or бавно.

days.title: Asking the way: directions

vocab.exampleEn: The station is to the left.
The stop is to the right.

grammar.title: Giving directions with place adverbs
grammar.explanation: Directions combine the prepositions you know (на, до, в) with adverbs of direction: наляво (to the left), надясно (to the right), направо (straight ahead), близо (near), далече (far), тук (here), там (there). These adverbs do not change form. To tell someone to go, use the imperative върви (go): Върви направо, после наляво.

examples.english: The stop is there, to the right.
Go straight ahead to the station.
The centre is near, to the left.

reading.title: Къде е гарата?
reading.text: — Извинете, къде е гарата? — Направо, после наляво. Близо е.
reading.translation: — Excuse me, where is the station? — Straight ahead, then to the left. It's near.

days.speakingPrompt: Give simple directions from your home to the nearest bus or tram stop using наляво, надясно and направо.

days.title: Putting a trip together

vocab.exampleEn: I am waiting at the stop.

grammar.title: Combining prepositions across a whole journey
grammar.explanation: Now chain the prepositions into one trip: от (from), за/на (to), с (by), до (next to/up to). Example: Тръгвам от центъра с автобус за гарата (I set off from the centre by bus to the station). All nouns stay unchanged; only definite suffixes and prepositions do the work. Time uses в: в три (at three).

examples.english: I set off from home by metro.
The train to Varna departs early.
I am waiting for the bus at the stop.

reading.title: На гарата
reading.text: — Влакът за Варна тръгва рано. — Да, в седем. Чакам на гарата.
reading.translation: — The train to Varna departs early. — Yes, at seven. I am waiting at the station.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe a short trip from start to finish, using от, с, за and a time with в.

days.title: Review: Getting around

grammar.title: Week recap: place and movement prepositions
grammar.explanation: This week you learned to get around without any noun cases — everything is done with prepositions before an unchanged noun. Place: в (in), на (on/at), до (next to). Movement: от (from), за (to), с (by means of). Plus пеша (on foot) with no preposition, and direction adverbs наляво, надясно, направо, близо, далече, тук, там. Remember to use the verb 'to be' (е, са) for descriptions and да + present for 'want to go' (искам да отида).

examples.russian: Пътувам с влак от София за Варна.
examples.english: I travel by train from Sofia to Varna.
The stop is next to the station, to the left.

examples.russian: Искам да отида в центъра пеша.
examples.english: I want to go to the centre on foot.

reading.title: Накъде?
reading.text: — Здравей! С какво отиваш в центъра? — С автобус. Спирката е до гарата. — А аз отивам пеша, близо е.
reading.translation: — Hi! How are you going to the centre? — By bus. The stop is next to the station. — And I am going on foot, it's near.

days.speakingPrompt: Have a short dialogue: ask a friend how they get to the centre and answer, using at least three prepositions from this week (в, на, до, от, за, с).
